title: Selecting and Sorting Data for Printing
breadcrumbs: Selecting and Sorting Data for Printing
text: Selecting and Sorting Data for Printing Selecting and sorting your data before printing is critical to good reports. If it is not feasible to select and sort your data before printing, you can also manipulate occurrences during printing, although this is less efficient. Selecting Data The component data structure provides the initial data selection, but further selection criteria are required to retrieve, for example, all employees with a certain salary or higher. In a form component, the user can enter a retrieve profile to select the data. In a report component, the selection criteria must be provided using ProcScript. Use the u_where clause with the read statement in the read trigger of the entity concerned, specifying the retrieve profile you require. For example: trigger read ; of entity INVOICE read u_where (INVAMOUNT > 500) order by "INVAMOUNT" end; read Copy Sorting Data Data sorting plays an important role in the purpose of a report. For example, a report about unpaid invoices which are sorted by date has a different purpose to one in which the invoices are sorted by amount. When the data in your report does not come from the database in the required order, use the order by clause with the read statement in the read trigger of the entity concerned, specifying the field or fields you want to sort by. Alternatively, use the sort statement. Below is an example of the order by clause in ProcScript in the read trigger: trigger read ; of entity INVOICE read order by "INVAMOUNT" end; read Copy Note: Ascending and descending sorting is not always possible with the order by clause for all DBMSs. See the appropriate DBMS connector documentation about your DBMS. If your data is already in the correct order when it reaches Uniface, do not use an order by clause. This is an unnecessary use of resources which slows the procedure down considerably.
